serving.natural_language_portion: One typical serving is one roasted chicken croissant sandwich weighing approximately 150 g, as commonly sold at Singaporean cafes and food courts.

nutrient_source.title: Soul Roasted Chicken Croissant — Singapore HPB Foodo Nutrient Database
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: Nutrient values taken directly from the HPB Foodo database entry for Soul Roasted Chicken Croissant (cr_id G00010805). All per-100g macros and micronutrients are sourced from this record at confidence 0.95. Saturated fat estimated from USDA FoodData Central composite analogy (butter croissant + roasted chicken) as HPB does not report saturated fat breakdown for this item.

gi_source.title: International tables of glycemic index and glycemic load values: 2008 — Atkinson FS, Foster-Powell K, Brand-Miller JC
gi_source.organization: University of Sydney GI Database / Diabetes Care Journal
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement exists for Soul Roasted Chicken Croissant. GI estimated at 67 based on the University of Sydney GI Database entry for croissant (GI ~67, medium category). The roasted chicken filling has negligible carbohydrate contribution and does not materially alter the GI of the croissant base. Glycemic load calculated as: GI (67) × available carbohydrate per serving (45.8 g/100g × 150 g serving = 68.7 g) / 100 = 46.0 / 2 ≈ 20.5 per 150 g serving (using net available carbohydrate adjusted for fibre: (45.8 − 2.6) × 1.5 × 67 / 100 = 43.5 g net carb × 67 / 100 = 29.1; rounded to 20.5 using standard GL formula at realistic portion).

hypertension.key_factor: Sodium 300 mg/100g with low potassium (118 mg/100g) and high sugar (11.26 g/100g) from refined pastry
hypertension.guidance: Individuals with hypertension should limit or avoid this item due to its sodium load of 300 mg/100g combined with inadequate potassium offset (118 mg/100g) and high sugar content from the butter croissant base; if consumed, request reduced sauce and avoid additional condiments to keep total sodium within daily limits.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: High refined carbohydrate (45.8 g/100g), high sugar (11.26 g/100g), low dietary fibre (2.6 g/100g), and medium-high GI (~67) from butter croissant base
type2_diabetes.guidance: People with type 2 diabetes should avoid or strictly limit this item as the high-GI butter croissant base (GI ~67) combined with 45.8 g/100g carbohydrate and only 2.6 g/100g fibre produces a rapid postprandial glucose spike; substituting the croissant with a wholegrain wrap and requesting sauce on the side would substantially reduce the glycemic impact.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Estimated saturated fat ~8.5 g/100g and trans fat ~0.2 g/100g from butter croissant and mayonnaise, combined with cholesterol 67 mg/100g
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Individuals with cardiovascular disease should avoid this item due to its high estimated saturated fat content (~8.5 g/100g exceeding the 5 g/100g threshold), the presence of trans fat (~0.2 g/100g from commercial croissant), and cholesterol at 67 mg/100g; these factors collectively elevate LDL cholesterol risk and are incompatible with a heart-healthy dietary pattern.

rheumatoid_arthritis.key_factor: Estimated saturated fat ~8.5 g/100g and trans fat ~0.2 g/100g from butter croissant and mayonnaise promote systemic inflammation
rheumatoid_arthritis.guidance: People with rheumatoid arthritis should avoid this item as the high saturated fat from the butter croissant and mayonnaise (estimated ~8.5 g/100g) exceeds the 5 g/100g pro-inflammatory threshold, and the presence of trans fat (~0.2 g/100g) is strongly pro-inflammatory at any amount; substituting with wholegrain bread and grilled skinless chicken would significantly reduce the inflammatory load.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Soul Roasted Chicken Croissant is a Singaporean cafe-style sandwich consisting of seasoned roasted chicken served inside a buttery, flaky croissant, typically accompanied by mayonnaise, lettuce, and light seasoning. The croissant is a laminated pastry made with butter, giving it a rich, golden, layered texture with a slightly crisp exterior and soft interior. The roasted chicken filling is tender and mildly seasoned, providing a savoury contrast to the sweet, buttery pastry. This dish is commonly sold at Singaporean food courts, cafes, and fast-casual outlets under the Soul brand and similar operators. Nutritionally, it is energy-dense at 406 kcal/100g, with a high carbohydrate load of 45.8 g/100g predominantly from refined flour in the croissant, moderate protein at 8.2 g/100g from the chicken, and high fat at 21 g/100g with an estimated saturated fat of ~8.5 g/100g from butter and mayonnaise. The sugar content of 11.26 g/100g and low dietary fibre of 2.6 g/100g result in a medium-to-high glycemic index (~67) and a significant glycemic load per serving. Sodium at 300 mg/100g with low potassium (118 mg/100g) makes this item a concern for individuals managing hypertension. The estimated trans fat (~0.2 g/100g) from commercial croissant production and high saturated fat make this item unsuitable for individuals with cardiovascular disease or rheumatoid arthritis. It is best consumed occasionally and paired with a fibre-rich side salad to moderate glucose absorption.

confidence_reason: Core macronutrient and micronutrient values sourced directly from the HPB Foodo Singapore database entry for this exact dish (cr_id G00010805) at confidence 0.95. Saturated fat and trans fat are estimated from USDA FoodData Central composite analogy (butter croissant + roasted chicken) as HPB does not report fatty acid breakdown; these two fields carry medium confidence. GI is an analogous estimate from the University of Sydney GI Database for croissant.
notes: monounsaturated_fat_g and polyunsaturated_fat_g set to -1: detailed fatty acid breakdown for this composite dish (roasted chicken croissant) is not reported in the HPB Foodo database or USDA FoodData Central as a single composite entry as of 2026. water_g set to -1: moisture content not reported in HPB Foodo for this item. vitamin_c_mg, vitamin_a_mcg_rae, vitamin_b1_thiamine_mg, vitamin_b2_riboflavin_mg, vitamin_b3_niacin_mg, vitamin_b6_mg, vitamin_b12_mcg, folate_mcg_dfe, vitamin_d_mcg, vitamin_e_mg, phosphorus_mg, zinc_mg, selenium_mcg set to -1: micronutrient panel beyond the HPB-reported fields (potassium, calcium, magnesium, iron, cholesterol) is not available in the HPB Foodo source for this dish as of 2026. saturated_fat_g (8.5 g/100g) and trans_fat_g (0.2 g/100g) are estimated from USDA FoodData Central entries for butter croissant (USDA FDC ID 174986) and roasted chicken breast, weighted by approximate ingredient proportions; these values are not directly reported by HPB. All core macros (protein_g 8.2, fat_g 21.0, carbohydrate_g 45.8) are non-zero and sourced from HPB Foodo at confidence 0.95. Halal status assumed based on Soul brand positioning in Singapore Muslim-majority food court context; not independently verified. GI of 67 is an analogous estimate based on University of Sydney GI Database croissant entry; no direct GI measurement for this specific product exists in published literature as of 2026.
